# Resource: ec_serverless_traffic_filter



## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "ec_serverless_traffic_filter" "example" {
  name        = "my-serverless-traffic-filter"
  region      = "aws-us-east-1"
  type        = "ip"
  description = "Allow traffic from the office network"

  include_by_default = false

  rules {
    source      = "203.0.113.0/24"
    description = "Office egress"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the traffic filter
- `region` (String) The traffic filter can be attached only to projects in the specific region
- `type` (String) Type of the traffic filter

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description of the traffic filter
- `include_by_default` (Boolean) Should the traffic filter be automatically included in the new projects
- `rules` (Attributes List) List of rules (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--rules))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The traffic filter ID

<a id="nestedatt--rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rules`

Required:

- `source` (String) Allowed traffic filter source: IP address, CIDR mask, or VPC endpoint ID

Optional:

- `description` (String) Description of the rule.

## Import

Serverless traffic filters can be imported using the `id`, for example:

```shell
terraform import ec_serverless_traffic_filter.id 320b7b540dfc967a7a649c18e2fce4ed
```

~> **Note on Credentials** The `credentials` attribute (containing `username` and `password`) is only available when the project is first created. When importing an existing project, these credentials will not be available in the Terraform state as the API does not return them on read operations.
